---
title: "The Viz Gallery: Community-Driven Art"
---

The core philosophy of SPARMVET is that visualizations shouldn't be locked behind complex code. The **Viz Gallery** is the beating heart of this philosophy.

## Purpose of the Gallery

The Gallery serves two massive purposes for the community (be it biologists, economists, or data analysts):
1. **Inspiration:** Not sure what plot best suits your data? Browse the gallery to see what peers have built and discover new analytical angles.
2. **Instant Recipes:** Every plot in the Gallery isn't just a picture; it's a full *recipe*. You can copy the exact YAML logic used to build that plot and paste it into your own manifest.

## Submitting to the Gallery

If you build a plot in the dashboard that you are proud of, we emphatically encourage you to submit it to the Gallery. If the gallery grows and is useful for people, it will ultimately benefit everybody using it to make it even better.

### Requirements for Submission
Before you submit, your Manifest must contain specific metadata to ensure you are credited and the context is preserved:

1. **Authors & Citations:** Provide your name, your team's name, or the publication citation if this plot was used in a manuscript. This allows the community to acknowledge and cite your work.
2. **Clear Node Comments:** In the "Wrangle Studio", every action node you created MUST have a comment explaining *why* you did it (e.g. "Filtered out calves because they skewed the baseline"). This teaches others how to think about their data.
3. **Data Agnostic Aim:** Try to label your plots in a way that others can understand, even if they aren't studying the exact same pathogen or economic model.

### Submission Process
1. Use the SPARMVET Dashboard's **Upload Data/Manifests** section.
2. Upload your finished project.
3. An administrator or backend automation will validate that the metadata rules are followed, and append your visualization to the global `VizFactory (viz_factory.py)` library, making it permanently accessible to the entire organization in the gallery tab!

---

## Gallery Taxonomy & Sidebar Filters

Every recipe is classified across **six axes**. These axes power the sidebar filter panels in the Gallery tab — selecting a value narrows the recipe list in real time.

| Icon | Axis | What it captures |
|------|------|-----------------|
| 📊 | **Family (Purpose)** | The analytical purpose: `Comparison`, `Distribution`, `Correlation`, `Evolution`, `Part-to-Whole`, `Ranking` |
| 🔢 | **Data Pattern** | The shape of the input data (e.g. `1 Numeric, 1 Categorical`) |
| 📈 | **Difficulty** | `Simple` / `Intermediate` / `Advanced` |
| ⚙️ | **Primary Geom** | The main plotnine layer (e.g. `geom_violin`, `geom_bar`, `geom_point`) |
| 🎯 | **What it Shows** | The chart's primary message: `distribution`, `trend`, `ranking`, `relationship`, … |
| 📏 | **Sample Size** | Whether the recipe needs large n (`large`, `medium+`) or works on any aggregated data (`any`) |

Authors must populate all six fields in the `info` block of their `recipe_manifest.yaml` before a submission is accepted. Full allowed-value lists, the manifest block format, and the `recipe_meta.md` tag-strip convention are in the [VizFactory Component Gallery appendix](../appendix/viz_factory_components.qmd#gallery-taxonomy-6-axis-classification).
